expected_properties: When din_valid is deasserted, changes on din_serial must not affect dout_parallel or dout_valid.
additional_cases.goal: Verify that the design ignores serial data when din_valid is low, preserving output stability.
notes: Ensures that the valid handshake semantics are respected despite the lack of an explicit ready signal.
preconditions: Design has been reset and is in a known idle state.
stimulus_intent: Toggle din_serial with din_valid low for several cycles, then assert din_valid and provide a valid 8‑bit sequence.
timing_assumptions: Observe that dout_parallel and dout_valid remain unchanged during the din_valid low period.
After din_valid is asserted, expect dout_valid to become high after the eighth valid bit.

expected_properties: The module should not assert dout_valid until exactly eight din_valid‑qualified bits have been received.
dout_valid must be deasserted when a new sequence starts.
additional_cases.goal: Exercise the implicit valid‑only protocol and verify correct assertion/deassertion of dout_valid.
notes: Addresses the contract ambiguity regarding missing ready signals.
preconditions: Module is out of reset and dout_valid is low.
stimulus_intent: Provide a continuous stream of din_serial bits with din_valid high, then insert a gap (din_valid low) before starting a new 8‑bit word.
timing_assumptions: Do not assume a fixed latency; monitor dout_valid for the first time it becomes high after each 8‑bit group.

expected_properties: A single 8‑bit valid sequence results in dout_parallel reflecting the transmitted bits (MSB first) and dout_valid asserting high after the last bit.
If din_valid is deasserted before eight bits are collected, dout_valid remains low.
baseline_case_enrichments.goal: Confirm correct accumulation of serial bits into the parallel word and proper valid signaling under nominal conditions.
notes: Stimulus respects the ambiguous ready semantics by keeping din_valid high throughout the sequence.
stimulus_intent: Drive a known 8‑bit pattern (e.g., 8'b1011_0110) on din_serial with din_valid asserted for each bit, synchronized to clk.
timing_assumptions: Observe dout_parallel and dout_valid after at least one rising edge following the eighth valid input; no exact-cycle latency is assumed.

planning_notes: All added cases use only ports declared in the contract.
Timing assumptions remain conservative due to the unknown latency model.
Negative and protocol cases address the contract's valid‑only ambiguities without inventing ready signals.
unresolved_items: Valid‑ready ambiguity for din_valid and dout_valid remains unresolved; cases are designed to be safe under either interpretation.
